pi_items.source: Confirmed by setup_task.sh which creates /home/ga/Desktop directory and expects file at this path
Chemical data from web search: 16.6°C = 61.9°F, rounded to 62°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, chemsrc.com
Chemical data from web search: 118.1°C = 244.6°F, rounded to 244°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, chemsrc.com
Melting point 62°F > threshold 41°F triggers HEAT action per task logic
Chemical data from web search: -123.5°C = -190.3°F, rounded to -190°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov
Chemical data from web search: 21°C = 69.8°F, rounded to 69°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, NIST webbook
Boiling point 69°F < threshold 95°F triggers COOL action per task logic
Chemical data from web search: 5.5°C = 41.9°F, rounded to 42°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, CAS Registry
Chemical data from web search: 80.1°C = 176.2°F, rounded to 176°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, CAS Registry
Melting point 42°F > threshold 41°F triggers HEAT action per task logic
Chemical data from web search: -145.9°C = -230.6°F, rounded to -231°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, SCBT, ChemSynthesis
Chemical data from web search: 34.0°C = 93.2°F, rounded to 93°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, PubChem
Boiling point 93°F < threshold 95°F triggers COOL action per task logic
Chemical data from web search: -114°C = -173.2°F, rounded to -173°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, Chemsrc, CommonOrganicChemistry
Chemical data from web search: 78.3°C = 172.9°F, rounded to 173°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, Wikipedia
Melting point -173°F < 41°F (not HEAT), boiling point 173°F > 95°F (not COOL), so STANDARD applies
Chemical data from web search: 13.2°C = 55.8°F, rounded to 56°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, chemsrc.com, CDC NIOSH
Chemical data from web search: 138.35°C = 280.6°F, rounded to 281°F. Source: chemicalbook.com, NIST webbook
Melting point 56°F > threshold 41°F triggers HEAT action per task logic
Task description specifies: Winter Min: 41°F (5°C). If Melting Point > 41°F -> Action: HEAT
Task description specifies: Summer Max: 95°F (35°C). If Boiling Point < 95°F -> Action: COOL

privileged_info_summary: All ground truth chemical properties are accurate and verified against authoritative sources. Melting and boiling points for all 6 chemicals (Acetic Acid, Acetaldehyde, Benzene, Isoprene, Ethanol, p-Xylene) match values from NOAA CAMEO Chemicals, ChemicalBook, NIST, and other chemical databases within standard rounding tolerances. Action classifications (HEAT, COOL, STANDARD) are correctly computed using the specified thresholds: HEAT if melting point > 41°F, COOL if boiling point < 95°F, otherwise STANDARD. Output path is correctly specified as /home/ga/Desktop/shipping_manifest.csv. Data source is CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/), a real public government database, not synthetic.
